wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the SmartPark AI project overview section. Explain the project's purpose as an AI-powered smart parking solution for UAE, its core architecture combining real-time IoT sensor data with predictive analytics and voice assistance. Document the technology stack including Next.js frontend, FastAPI backend, SQLite database, and WebSocket communication. Describe the system's key capabilities: real-time spot availability tracking, predictive occupancy forecasting, intelligent navigation, and solar-powered IoT sensor integration. Include high-level architecture diagrams showing component interactions between frontend, backend, database, and IoT devices. Address the target use cases for Dubai Internet City parking management and explain how the system solves urban parking challenges through AI and IoT technologies.

wiki_catalogs.name: FastAPI Application Setup
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the FastAPI application setup in SmartPark AI. Detail the application initialization process including lifespan management with async context managers for database initialization, data seeding, and background task lifecycle. Document CORS middleware configuration for cross-origin requests, router registration patterns for modular API organization, and WebSocket endpoint mounting. Explain the application structure with title, description, version metadata, and root endpoint implementation. Include error handling strategies, logging configuration, and performance optimization techniques specific to FastAPI applications. Provide examples of extending the application with additional routers and middleware components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Next.js Application Structure
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Next.js application structure focused on SmartPark AI's frontend architecture. Explain the app router configuration, metadata management, and global layout composition. Document the Providers pattern implementation for context management and global state initialization. Detail the page routing system with file-based routing conventions and dynamic routes. Include the global CSS configuration with Tailwind CSS setup and custom theme variables. Explain the HTML document structure, SEO optimization, and accessibility considerations. Provide examples of adding new pages, configuring layouts, and managing global styles. Address performance optimizations like code splitting and image optimization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Zones API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the Zones management endpoints. Document all REST endpoints including GET /api/zones (list all zones with computed spot counts), GET /api/zones/{zone_id} (get single zone with detailed spot information), and GET /api/zones/nearby (find zones within radius using Haversine distance calculation). For each endpoint, specify HTTP methods, URL patterns, query parameters (lat, lng, radius_m), request/response schemas using ZoneOut and ZoneDetailOut models, and error handling. Include practical examples with curl commands showing how to retrieve zones, filter by proximity, and get detailed zone information with spot status breakdowns. Document the geographic calculations, pricing structure fields, and spot count aggregations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Schema
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive database schema documentation for SmartPark AI. Detail the complete relational database structure including all entities: Zone, Spot, Sensor, Prediction, ParkEvent, and SavedPlace. Document primary keys, foreign key relationships, data types, constraints, and indexes for each table. Explain entity relationships such as one-to-many (Zone-Spot), one-to-one (Spot-Sensor), and many-to-one (Spot-ParkEvent) associations. Include field definitions with validation rules, default values, and nullable constraints. Document the SQLAlchemy ORM model definitions and their corresponding database table structures. Provide sample SQL queries for common operations and explain query optimization strategies. Address database migration patterns, versioning approaches, and backup/restore procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Interactive Demo System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Interactive Demo System that provides standalone simulation without backend dependencies. Document the real-time parking spot simulation with 48 spots across 3 zones (A, B, C), status transitions between free/occupied/reserved states, and configurable simulation speed controls. Explain the spot state management system, random occupancy algorithms, and user interaction capabilities including spot clicking to change states. Detail the visual feedback system with animations, color coding, and live indicators. Include configuration options for simulation parameters, zone layouts, and integration patterns for connecting to actual backend services.

wiki_catalogs.name: Engine Configuration & Connection Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the SQLAlchemy async engine configuration and connection management. Detail the async engine setup using create_async_engine, including database URL configuration with environment variables, connection pooling settings, and echo mode for debugging. Explain the async_sessionmaker configuration with expire_on_commit behavior and session lifecycle management. Document the Base declarative base class inheritance pattern and its role in ORM model definitions. Include examples of database initialization using init_db function, connection testing patterns, and error handling strategies. Provide guidance on configuring different database backends (SQLite, PostgreSQL, MySQL) and production deployment considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Context Providers
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the SmartPark AI Context Provider architecture. Explain how React Context is used to manage global application state across component trees. Document the VoiceProvider implementation for voice agent state management, including speech recognition state, conversation history, and real-time audio processing. Detail the PlacesContext for managing saved parking locations, CRUD operations, and local storage persistence. Include practical examples of creating custom providers, consuming context in components, and handling provider composition. Explain the benefits of using Context over prop drilling, performance considerations with useContext, and best practices for provider structure. Address error boundaries, loading states, and data synchronization patterns within providers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Map Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the SmartPark AI map visualization components. Detail the ParkingMap component as the main container, SpotMarker for individual parking spot visualization with status indicators, ZonePolygon for geographic boundaries and pricing zones, SearchBar for location search functionality, MapControls for zoom and view controls, CurrentLocation for user positioning, SavedPlaceMarker for bookmarked locations, and SpotSheet for detailed spot information display. Document Leaflet integration patterns, coordinate systems, marker clustering, polygon rendering, and real-time updates. Include prop interfaces, event handling, styling customization, and performance optimization techniques for large datasets.

wiki_catalogs.name: REST API Client
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the REST API client implementation in SmartPark AI. Detail the fetch-based HTTP client abstraction with request/response handling, error management, and configuration options. Document all available API methods including fetchZones(), fetchPredictions(), and sendAgentQuery() with their parameters, return types, and usage patterns. Explain the base URL configuration using NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL environment variable. Include concrete examples of making API calls, handling loading states, and managing errors. Document the response data structures and type safety considerations. Address CORS configuration, request headers, and content-type handling. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common network issues and debugging techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Interactive Mapping
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for SmartPark AI's interactive mapping system built with Leaflet. Explain the map initialization, tile layer configuration with CARTO dark theme, and coordinate system setup for Dubai location. Document custom spot markers with status-based coloring (free/occupied/reserved/sensor_offline), zone polygon overlays with real-time availability ratios, and user location tracking. Detail the search functionality with geolocation autocomplete, place finding with smooth fly-to animations, and saved places management. Include map controls for simulation playback, speed adjustment, and zoom operations. Explain the spot selection system with bottom sheet display, event handling patterns, and state synchronization. Address performance optimization for large datasets, memory management for marker clusters, and responsive design considerations. Provide implementation examples for adding new marker types, customizing map styles, and integrating with external mapping services.

wiki_catalogs.name: ParkingMap Component
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the ParkingMap component, the main container for the interactive parking map visualization. Document its role as the central orchestrator that manages state for spots, selected spot information, and simulation controls. Explain the Leaflet MapContainer integration with custom tile layers, coordinate system setup (Dubai coordinates), and zoom configuration. Detail the component's prop interfaces including center coordinates, zoom level, and tile layer configuration. Document event handling patterns for spot selection, simulation control, and search functionality. Include implementation details for real-time updates through the useSimulator hook, spot filtering by zone, and performance optimization techniques for rendering large datasets. Provide examples of customization options, styling approaches, and integration with other map components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Speech Recognition Engine
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed content for the speech recognition engine focusing on Web Speech API integration and audio processing. Explain the useVoiceAgent hook implementation for managing speech-to-text conversion, microphone access permissions, and real-time audio capture. Document the VoiceButton component with recording states (idle, listening, processing), error handling for permission denials, and accessibility features including keyboard navigation and screen reader support. Include browser compatibility considerations, fallback mechanisms for unsupported browsers, and performance optimization techniques for continuous speech recognition. Provide implementation examples for custom voice commands, audio quality settings, and integration patterns with the backend agent service.

wiki_catalogs.name: Map Core & Initialization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the core map initialization and configuration system. Explain the Leaflet map setup with React-Leaflet wrapper, including center coordinates for Dubai location (25.092, 55.160), zoom level configuration, and tile layer integration with CARTO dark theme. Document the map container component structure, ref management for programmatic control, and event handling patterns. Detail the coordinate system setup, map bounds configuration, and performance optimizations for large datasets. Include examples of customizing map styles, adding new tile providers, and implementing map lifecycle methods. Address responsive design considerations and cross-browser compatibility issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Prediction Chart Component
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the PredictionChart component that renders time-series occupancy forecasts using Chart.js. Explain the chart configuration including dual-line visualization with actual vs predicted data, confidence interval rendering, and interactive tooltips. Document the data generation algorithm that creates realistic parking occupancy patterns based on time-of-day profiles, including the seeded pseudo-random noise system for consistent demo data. Detail the responsive design implementation with mobile-optimized touch interactions and performance considerations for smooth animations. Include examples of customizing chart appearance, adding new datasets, and integrating with real-time data updates. Address error handling for missing data points and accessibility features like keyboard navigation and screen reader support.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for SmartPark AI. Provide step-by-step installation instructions for both quick start using the automated script and manual setup procedures. Document environment prerequisites including Python version, Node.js requirements, and system dependencies. Include detailed backend setup with virtual environment creation, dependency installation, database seeding, and server startup commands. Cover frontend development environment setup with npm installation and development server configuration. Document access URLs for frontend (port 3000), backend API (port 8000), and interactive API documentation. Include troubleshooting guidance for common setup issues, port conflicts, and dependency problems. Provide verification steps to ensure successful installation and basic functionality testing.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Layer
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the SQLAlchemy-based database layer architecture. Document the async engine configuration, connection pooling settings, and session management patterns using dependency injection. Explain the base model inheritance structure and common field definitions across all entities. Detail the ORM model relationships including foreign keys, backreferences, and cascade operations. Document Pydantic schema definitions for request/response validation and serialization. Include database migration strategies, indexing approaches, and query optimization techniques. Provide examples of complex queries, relationship navigation, and transaction management patterns used throughout the application.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Library
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the SmartPark AI component library organization and architecture. Thoroughly explain the component hierarchy including map visualization components (ParkingMap, SpotMarker, ZonePolygon), voice interface components (VoiceAgentWidget, ChatPanel, VoiceButton), prediction analytics components (PredictionPanel, PredictionChart), place management components (PlacesList, PlaceCard), and landing page components (Hero, ArchitectureSection). Document component composition patterns, prop interfaces, and event handling mechanisms. Include concrete examples from the actual codebase showing component usage and customization options. Explain the relationship between parent and child components, data flow patterns, and state lifting strategies. Address component reusability guidelines, naming conventions, and folder organization principles.

wiki_catalogs.name: Spots API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for the Spots tracking endpoints. Document all REST endpoints for managing parking spots including CRUD operations, status updates, and real-time monitoring capabilities. Specify HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas using SpotOut model, authentication requirements, and error codes. Include examples for updating spot status (free/occupied/reserved/sensor_offline), retrieving spot details, and bulk operations where applicable. Document the relationship between spots and zones, sensor associations, and timestamp tracking for occupancy changes.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Schemas & Validation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API schema documentation for SmartPark AI backend. Document all Pydantic models used for request/response validation and serialization across REST endpoints. Detail schema definitions for Zone creation/update requests, Spot status updates, Prediction queries, and Sensor management operations. Include field validation rules, data type constraints, and business logic validation. Document error response formats, validation error handling, and custom validators. Provide examples of valid and invalid request payloads with expected responses. Address schema versioning strategies, backward compatibility considerations, and migration paths for API changes. Include authentication and authorization schemas where applicable.

wiki_catalogs.name: Real-time Parking Map
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Real-time Parking Map system built with Leaflet.js integration. Document the interactive map components including custom spot markers with status-based styling, zone polygon overlays with geographic boundaries, and real-time location updates via WebSocket connections. Explain the search functionality with place autocomplete, current location detection, and navigation integration. Detail the marker clustering system, zoom level optimizations, and responsive design considerations. Include configuration options for map providers, custom themes, and integration with external mapping services like Google Maps or Apple Maps.

wiki_catalogs.name: ORM Models & Entity Definitions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the SQLAlchemy ORM models and entity definitions. Document each domain model including Zone, Spot, Sensor, Prediction, and ParkEvent with their field definitions, data types, and constraints. Explain relationship mappings including foreign keys, backreferences, and cascade operations between entities. Detail indexing strategies, unique constraints, and validation rules applied at the model level. Include examples of common query patterns, relationship navigation, and transaction management. Document model inheritance patterns if used and explain how models integrate with Pydantic schemas for API serialization. Provide best practices for model design, performance optimization, and migration strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Custom Hooks
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for SmartPark AI custom hooks architecture. Thoroughly explain the hook composition patterns and state management strategies. Document useVoiceAgent hook implementation including speech recognition lifecycle, transcript processing, reasoning step visualization, and error handling. Detail useSimulator hook for real-time parking spot simulation, Dubai time zone awareness, and WebSocket integration. Include concrete examples of hook dependencies, cleanup functions, and performance optimization techniques like useCallback and useMemo. Explain async state management, side effect handling, and cross-hook communication patterns. Address testing strategies for custom hooks, TypeScript integration, and debugging techniques. Provide guidelines for creating reusable hooks and maintaining consistent patterns across the codebase.

wiki_catalogs.name: Voice Interface Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the SmartPark AI voice interface components. Explain the VoiceAgentWidget as the main entry point, ChatPanel for conversation display, VoiceButton for activation control, VoiceOverlay for modal presentation, VoiceProvider for state management context, Waveform for audio visualization, SuggestionChips for quick actions, ReasoningSteps for AI transparency, and MapCard for location suggestions. Document speech recognition integration, audio processing, WebSocket communication, state synchronization, accessibility features, and responsive design patterns. Include usage examples, customization options, and error handling strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: WebSocket Client
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the WebSocket client implementation in SmartPark AI. Thoroughly explain the real-time spot updates system using createSpotsWebSocket() function. Document connection establishment, automatic protocol switching from HTTP to WS, and connection lifecycle management. Detail message formats, event handling patterns, and real-time data synchronization strategies. Include concrete examples of connecting to the WebSocket endpoint, handling incoming spot status updates, and managing connection errors. Explain the server-side WebSocket architecture at /ws/spots endpoint. Address connection retry logic, reconnection strategies, and graceful degradation when WebSocket is unavailable. Document testing approaches for real-time features and mocking strategies for development.

wiki_catalogs.name: Voice Assistant Interface
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed content for SmartPark AI's voice assistant interface focusing on speech recognition, audio processing, and conversational UI. Explain the Web Speech API integration for speech-to-text conversion, microphone access permissions, and audio waveform visualization with real-time feedback. Document the chat panel implementation with message history, typing indicators, and conversation context management. Detail the voice button component with recording states, error handling, and accessibility features. Include the overlay system for full-screen voice interactions, modal management, and background audio processing. Explain the voice provider architecture for global state management, connection lifecycle, and error recovery. Address browser compatibility issues, permission handling, and fallback mechanisms for unsupported browsers. Provide implementation examples for custom voice commands, response formatting, and integration with the backend agent service.

wiki_catalogs.name: SpotMarker Component
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the SpotMarker component responsible for visualizing individual parking spots on the map. Document the marker rendering logic based on spot status (free, occupied, reserved, sensor_offline) with appropriate color coding and visual indicators. Explain the Leaflet marker implementation, icon customization, and click event handling. Detail the prop interface including spot data structure, click callbacks, and styling options. Document the integration with the simulation system for real-time status updates and animation effects. Include examples of custom marker icons, hover states, and accessibility considerations. Address performance optimization for rendering many markers and clustering strategies for large datasets.

wiki_catalogs.name: Audio Waveform Visualization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed content for the audio waveform visualization system focusing on real-time audio feedback and visual indicators. Explain the Waveform component implementation using Canvas API or SVG for rendering audio levels during speech recognition. Document the audio level detection algorithms, animation performance optimization, and responsive design considerations for different screen sizes. Include styling customization options, color schemes for different states (listening, processing, error), and accessibility features for users with visual impairments. Address browser-specific audio APIs, memory management for long-running audio sessions, and battery optimization techniques for mobile devices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Spot Markers System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the spot marker system that displays parking spot availability on the map. Explain the marker creation process using Leaflet markers with status-based coloring (free=green, occupied=red, reserved=blue, sensor_offline=gray). Document the marker click event handling, popup display logic, and state synchronization with the simulation engine. Detail the marker positioning using spot coordinates, icon customization options, and performance optimization for rendering hundreds of markers. Include implementation examples for adding new marker types, customizing marker appearances, and integrating with external marker libraries like MarkerCluster for better performance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Prediction Panel Interface
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the PredictionPanel component that serves as the main interface for predictive analytics display. Document the panel layout structure, data filtering capabilities, and real-time update mechanisms. Explain the integration with the backend prediction API, including request/response handling, error states, and loading indicators. Detail the user interaction patterns such as zone selection, time range filtering, and export functionality. Include implementation examples for adding new filter criteria, customizing panel appearance, and extending the data presentation formats. Address performance optimization techniques for large datasets, caching strategies, and memory management for long-running sessions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the SmartPark AI backend system. Describe the FastAPI-based microservices architecture with clear separation of concerns across routers, models, and business logic layers. Document the application initialization process, middleware configuration, CORS setup, and router registration patterns. Explain the async SQLAlchemy database layer design including connection pooling, session management, and base model inheritance. Detail the WebSocket implementation for real-time spot updates and connection management patterns. Document the agent system architecture for voice processing and natural language understanding. Include the simulation engine design for realistic parking scenario generation. Address error handling strategies, logging patterns, and performance optimization techniques. Provide system context diagrams showing component interactions and data flow patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: WebSocket Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the WebSocket real-time communication system. Document the connection manager implementation using pub/sub pattern for broadcasting spot status updates to connected clients. Explain connection lifecycle management including authentication, authorization, and graceful disconnection handling. Detail the message protocol design for spot updates, event types, and data synchronization patterns. Include client connection establishment, reconnection logic, and error recovery mechanisms. Document performance considerations for high-concurrency scenarios, memory management, and scaling strategies. Provide examples of implementing custom WebSocket endpoints and integrating with existing business logic.

wiki_catalogs.name: State Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the SmartPark AI state management architecture. Explain the multi-layered approach using React Context for global state (VoiceProvider, PlacesContext), custom hooks for local component logic (useVoiceAgent, useSimulator), and local storage for persistence. Document the provider pattern implementation for voice agent state, place management context, and real-time simulation state. Detail the hook composition patterns, dependency injection, and state synchronization strategies. Include practical examples of creating custom hooks, managing async state, and handling side effects. Explain the separation of concerns between different state layers and when to use each approach. Address performance considerations like memoization, selective updates, and memory management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Predictions API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the Predictions endpoints. Document the predictive analytics API that provides 12-hour occupancy forecasts with confidence scores. Specify HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas using PredictionOut model, and filtering options. Include examples for requesting predictions for specific zones or time periods, understanding confidence score interpretations, and handling prediction data structures. Document the underlying prediction algorithms, historical data usage, and forecast accuracy metrics.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Type Definitions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive TypeScript type definition documentation for SmartPark AI frontend. Document all interface and type definitions used throughout the React application for type safety and developer experience. Detail core domain types including Zone, Spot, Sensor, Prediction, and Place interfaces that mirror backend data models. Document API response types, request payload types, and utility types for state management. Include type guards, discriminated unions, and generic types used for flexible component APIs. Document WebSocket message types, event handlers, and real-time data structures. Provide examples of type usage in components, hooks, and API clients. Address type synchronization strategies between frontend and backend schemas and development workflow for maintaining type consistency.

wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the AI Voice Assistant featuring speech recognition, natural language processing, and contextual responses. Explain the chat interface with message history, suggestion chips, and reasoning steps display. Detail the backend agent router handling natural language queries, context resolution, and response generation. Include voice command examples, conversation flow patterns, and customization options for response templates and personality settings. Address browser compatibility, microphone permissions, and fallback text input methods.

wiki_catalogs.name: Pydantic Schemas & Data Validation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for Pydantic schema definitions and data validation patterns. Document request/response schemas for all API endpoints including validation rules, field constraints, and serialization configurations. Explain the relationship between SQLAlchemy models and Pydantic schemas, including conversion patterns and nested object handling. Detail custom validators, field transformations, and error handling strategies. Include examples of complex validation scenarios, conditional fields, and schema composition patterns. Document best practices for schema versioning, backward compatibility, and API contract management. wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for SmartPark AI local storage and data persistence strategies. Document the PlacesContext implementation for CRUD operations on saved parking locations, including data validation, serialization, and migration strategies. Detail the seed data system for initial application state and development scenarios. Include practical examples of safe localStorage usage, error handling for quota exceeded scenarios, and data backup strategies. Explain the separation between temporary runtime state and persistent data, caching strategies for API responses, and performance considerations for large datasets. Address security implications of client-side storage and data sanitization patterns.

Detail how the system processes natural language queries about parking availability, zone information, and navigation assistance. Include concrete examples of query-response pairs, context variables, and response customization options. Explain the integration with the voice agent widget and chat interface components. Address response caching strategies, conversation history management, and personalization features. Document the extensibility points for adding new response types and customizing agent behavior.

wiki_catalogs.name: Real-time Updates System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed content for SmartPark AI's real-time update system spanning both frontend and backend WebSocket communication. Explain the simulator hook implementation for generating realistic parking spot status changes, time-based algorithms, and Dubai timezone awareness. Document the WebSocket client architecture for connection management, automatic reconnection, and error handling. Detail the message protocol for spot status updates, zone availability changes, and sensor health notifications. Include the state synchronization mechanism between server and client, optimistic updates, and conflict resolution. Explain performance optimization techniques for high-frequency updates, memory management for large spot collections, and battery usage considerations for mobile devices. Address debugging tools for real-time data flow, logging strategies, and monitoring approaches. Provide implementation examples for adding new real-time features, custom event handlers, and offline support patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: SearchBar Component
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the SearchBar component providing location search functionality within the parking map interface. Document the search input handling, autocomplete suggestions, and place result processing. Explain the integration with geocoding services or local place databases, search algorithm implementation, and result ranking. Detail the prop interface including search callbacks, placeholder text, and styling customization. Document the user interaction flow from input to map navigation, including error handling for invalid searches and loading states. Include examples of custom search providers, result formatting, and keyboard navigation support. Address accessibility compliance, mobile responsiveness, and performance optimization for search operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Voice Overlay System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed content for the voice overlay system focusing on modal management and full-screen voice interactions. Explain the VoiceOverlay component implementation with backdrop handling, focus management, and escape key support. Document the overlay lifecycle including open/close animations, z-index management, and background audio processing continuation. Include integration with the voice provider for global state synchronization, click-outside-to-close functionality, and mobile viewport considerations. Address browser compatibility for overlay positioning, touch event handling, and performance optimization for smooth transitions between overlay states.

wiki_catalogs.name: Search & Navigation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the search and navigation functionality within the mapping system. Explain the geolocation autocomplete implementation using Nominatim API, search result processing, and place finding with smooth fly-to animations. Document the current location tracking system, user position display with custom markers, and location permission handling. Detail the search input validation, debounced API calls, and error handling for network failures. Include implementation examples for adding new search providers, customizing search results display, and integrating with external mapping services. Address privacy considerations for location data and fallback mechanisms when geolocation is unavailable.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the SmartPark AI frontend application. Describe the Next.js 15 application structure with React 19 components, TypeScript integration, and Tailwind CSS styling. Document the page routing system, layout composition, and global provider patterns for state management. Explain the component hierarchy including map visualization components, voice interface widgets, prediction charts, and landing page elements. Detail the state management approach using React Context, custom hooks, and local storage integration. Document the API client abstraction layer for REST and WebSocket communication. Include the Leaflet map integration with custom markers, polygon overlays, and real-time updates. Address responsive design patterns, accessibility considerations, and performance optimization strategies. Provide component composition guidelines and reusability patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Simulation Engine
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the simulation engine that generates realistic parking scenarios. Document the background task architecture using asyncio for continuous spot status changes and sensor health monitoring. Explain the probability algorithms for simulating parking occupancy patterns based on time of day, location characteristics, and historical trends. Detail the seed data generation system for creating initial test datasets with realistic parking zones, spots, and sensor configurations. Include the event-driven architecture for triggering real-time updates through WebSocket connections. Document configuration options for adjusting simulation parameters, performance tuning, and testing strategies. Provide examples of customizing simulation behavior and integrating with external data sources.

wiki_catalogs.name: Interactive Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for SmartPark AI's interactive features focusing on real-time user experiences. Explain the Leaflet map integration with custom markers, polygon overlays, clustering, and real-time updates. Document the voice assistant interface including speech recognition, audio waveform visualization, chat panel, and conversation management. Detail the predictive analytics visualization with Chart.js integration, interactive charts, and data filtering. Include the search and navigation features with geolocation, autocomplete, and route planning. Explain the responsive design patterns for mobile and desktop experiences. Address performance optimization for real-time updates, memory management for large datasets, and accessibility compliance for interactive elements. Provide implementation examples and troubleshooting guides for common issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Places API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the Places management endpoints. Document CRUD operations for saved locations and favorites including creating, updating, deleting, and listing user places. Specify HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas using PlaceOut model, authentication requirements, and user association. Include examples for adding frequently visited locations, organizing saved places, and retrieving place collections. Document the relationship between places and zones, coordinate storage, and metadata management.

wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Place Management System enabling saved locations, quick access, and route planning. Document the CRUD operations for parking places including creation through modal interfaces, editing capabilities, and deletion workflows. <blog>

# SmartPark AI - Comprehensive Project Analysis

## 1. Project Introduction

### Purpose Statement
SmartPark UAE is an AI-powered smart parking management system designed for Dubai Internet City, providing real-time spot availability tracking, predictive occupancy analysis, and intelligent navigation capabilities. The system combines IoT sensors, machine learning predictions, and a modern web interface to solve urban parking challenges.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Real-time Parking Management**: Track parking spot availability using IoT sensors
- **Predictive Analytics**: Use ML algorithms to forecast parking occupancy patterns
- **Intelligent Navigation**: Guide drivers to available parking spots efficiently
- **Voice Assistant Integration**: Provide natural language interaction through AI agents
- **IoT Hardware Integration**: Monitor sensor health and status from solar-powered devices

### Target Audience
- Urban parking facility managers in Dubai Internet City
- Drivers seeking efficient parking solutions
- Municipal authorities managing parking infrastructure
- IoT developers working with smart city technologies

## 2. Technical Architecture

The SmartPark AI system follows a modern microservices architecture with clear separation between frontend and backend concerns:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Frontend - Next.js] --> B[FastAPI Backend]
    B --> C[(SQLite Database)]
    B --> D[WebSocket Server]
    D --> E[Real-time Updates]
    B --> F[AI Voice Agent]
    B --> G[Prediction Engine]
    H[IoT Sensors] --> I[Sensor Data API]
    I --> B
    
    subgraph "Frontend Components"
        J[Interactive Map]
        K[Voice Agent Widget]
        L[Predictions Dashboard]
        M[Place Management]
    end
    
    subgraph "Backend Services"
        N[REST API Routers]
        O[WebSocket Manager]
        P[Database Layer]
        Q[Simulation Engine]
    end
    
    A --> J
    A --> K
    A --> L
    A --> M
    B --> N
    B --> O
    B --> P
    B --> Q
```

### Component Breakdown

**Frontend Architecture (Next.js 15)**
- **Page-based Routing**: Utilizes Next.js App Router for structured page organization
- **Component Composition**: Modular React components with TypeScript support
- **State Management**: Custom hooks and context providers for application state
- **Real-time Features**: WebSocket integration for live parking updates
- **Interactive Mapping**: Leaflet-based map components with custom markers

**Backend Architecture (FastAPI)**
- **Async-first Design**: Built on asyncio for high-performance concurrent operations
- **RESTful API**: Well-structured endpoints following REST conventions
- **WebSocket Support**: Real-time communication for live spot updates
- **Database Abstraction**: SQLAlchemy ORM with async SQLite support
- **Modular Routers**: Separated business logic into focused route modules

### Design Patterns

**Repository Pattern**: Database operations are abstracted through SQLAlchemy models
**Observer Pattern**: WebSocket manager implements pub/sub for real-time updates
**Factory Pattern**: Connection management for database sessions
**Strategy Pattern**: Different prediction algorithms can be swapped easily

### System Relationships

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant User as Frontend User
    participant Map as InteractiveMap
    participant WS as WebSocket Client
    participant API as FastAPI Server
    participant DB as SQLite Database
    participant Sim as Simulator
    
    User->>+Map: Click parking spot
    Map->>+WS: Request spot details
    WS->>+API: GET /api/spots/{id}
    API->>+DB: Query spot data
    DB-->>-API: Return spot info
    API-->>-WS: JSON response
    WS-->>-Map: Update UI
    Sim->>+API: Update spot status
    API->>+DB: Persist changes
    DB-->>-API: Confirm update
    API->>+WS: Broadcast change
    WS-->>-Map: Real-time update
```

## 3. Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Backend Entry Point** (`backend/main.py`)
- FastAPI application initialization with CORS middleware
- Application lifespan management for database initialization and simulator startup
- WebSocket endpoint registration at `/ws/spots`
- Modular router inclusion for different API domains

**Frontend Entry Point** (`frontend/src/app/layout.tsx`)
- Root layout configuration with metadata and global styles
- Voice agent widget integration across all pages
- Provider pattern implementation for global state management

### Core Modules

**Database Layer** (`backend/database.py`)
- Async SQLAlchemy engine configuration with SQLite
- Session management with connection pooling
- Base model class for declarative ORM definitions

**Data Models** (`backend/models.py`)
- Comprehensive domain models: Zone, Spot, Sensor, SavedPlace, Prediction, ParkEvent
- Relationship mappings with proper foreign key constraints
- Timestamp management with timezone-aware datetime handling

**WebSocket Management** (`backend/ws/__init__.py`)
- Connection manager for handling multiple client connections
- Broadcast functionality for real-time spot status updates
- Automatic connection cleanup and error handling

### Configuration Approach

**Environment Configuration** (`backend/database.py`)
- Database URL configuration via environment variables
- Default SQLite file fallback for development
- Configurable echo settings for debugging

**Build Configuration** (`frontend/package.json`, `backend/requirements.txt`)
- Modern dependency management with version pinning
- Development and production script separation
- TypeScript and ESLint configuration for code quality

### External Dependencies

**Backend Dependencies** (`backend/requirements.txt`)
- FastAPI 0.115.0: High-performance web framework
- SQLAlchemy 2.0.30: Async ORM with comprehensive features
- WebSockets 12.0: Real-time bidirectional communication
- Pydantic 2.7.0: Data validation and serialization

**Frontend Dependencies** (`frontend/package.json`)
- Next.js 16.2.10: React framework with server-side rendering
- React 19.2.4: Latest React with concurrent features
- Leaflet 1.9.4: Interactive mapping library
- Chart.js 4.5.1: Data visualization components

### Integration Points

**API Endpoints Structure** (`backend/routers/`)
- `/api/zones`: Zone management and queries
- `/api/spots`: Individual spot information and updates
- `/api/predict`: Machine learning prediction endpoints
- `/api/agent`: AI voice assistant integration
- `/api/places`: User saved locations management
- `/api/sensors`: IoT sensor monitoring and control

**Real-time Communication** (`backend/ws/`)
- WebSocket endpoint for live parking spot updates
- Connection lifecycle management
- Broadcast messaging to connected clients

### Component Relationships

```mermaid
classDiagram
    class ParkingMap {
        +spots: Spot[]
        +selectedSpot: Spot
        +handleSpotClick()
        +handleToggle()
    }
    
    class SpotMarker {
        +spot: Spot
        +onClick()
        +renderStatus()
    }
    
    class ZonePolygon {
        +zone: Zone
        +freeRatio: number
        +renderGeometry()
    }
    
    class VoiceAgentWidget {
        +isListening: boolean
        +toggleListening()
        +processCommand()
    }
    
    class ConnectionManager {
        +active_connections: WebSocket[]
        +connect()
        +disconnect()
        +broadcast()
    }
    
    ParkingMap --> SpotMarker
    ParkingMap --> ZonePolygon
    ParkingMap --> VoiceAgentWidget
    ConnectionManager --> WebSocket
```

## 4. Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Real-time Parking Management**
- Live spot availability tracking through WebSocket connections
- Interactive map visualization with color-coded spot status
- Automated simulation engine for testing and demonstration

**AI-Powered Voice Assistant**
- Natural language processing for parking queries
- Context-aware responses based on user location and preferences
- Visual chat interface with reasoning step explanations

**Predictive Analytics Dashboard**
- Historical parking pattern analysis
- Future occupancy predictions with confidence scores
- Zone comparison tools for optimal parking decisions

**IoT Sensor Integration**
- Solar-powered ESP32 sensors with VL53L1X distance measurement
- Battery level and signal strength monitoring
- Firmware version tracking and health diagnostics

### Implementation Highlights

**Interactive Demo System** (`frontend/src/components/landing/InteractiveDemo.tsx`)
- Self-contained parking simulation without backend dependencies
- Real-time spot status animation and user interaction
- Educational tool for demonstrating system capabilities

**Voice Agent Architecture** (`frontend/src/components/voice/`)
- Modular voice component system with provider pattern
- Speech-to-text and text-to-speech integration
- Contextual conversation management and state persistence

**Advanced Mapping Features** (`frontend/src/components/map/`)
- Custom marker rendering with dynamic status indicators
- Polygon overlays for parking zones with occupancy visualization
- Search functionality with geocoding integration

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Idle
    Idle --> Searching: User Input
    Searching --> Found: Location Identified
    Searching --> Error: No Results
    Found --> Navigating: Start Navigation
    Navigating --> Arrived: Destination Reached
    Arrived --> Parking: Find Spot
    Parking --> Success: Spot Available
    Parking --> Failed: No Availability
    Success --> [*]
    Failed --> Searching: Try Again
    Error --> Idle: Retry
```

The SmartPark AI system represents a comprehensive solution for modern urban parking challenges, combining cutting-edge technologies with practical usability. Its modular architecture ensures scalability while maintaining code clarity and maintainability.

---

**Sources:**
- [README.md](README.md)
- [main.py](backend/main.py)
- [models.py](backend/models.py)
- [database.py](backend/database.py)
- [package.json](frontend/package.json)
- [requirements.txt](backend/requirements.txt)
- [layout.tsx](frontend/src/app/layout.tsx)
- [page.tsx](frontend/src/app/page.tsx)
- [spots.py](backend/routers/spots.py)
- [__init__.py](backend/ws/__init__.py)
- [ParkingMap.tsx](frontend/src/components/map/ParkingMap.tsx)
- [VoiceAgentWidget.tsx](frontend/src/components/voice/VoiceAgentWidget.tsx)

</blog>
